summary: No-ship: the slice documents the HOT/config gap only in run notes, not the fitting README required by acceptance, and the new vitest gives weak confidence because it mostly asserts a hand-built pool plus brittle source-string checks.

findings.title: Required fitting README documentation is missing
findings.recommendation: Do not mark s2 passed until the actual fitting README documents the HOT/BOOT contract and the no-/config gap. The diff only adds run-level/autothing notes; add the operator-facing section to the relevant fitting README and keep the run notes secondary.

findings.title: Warm-pool test does not exercise the production pool definition
findings.recommendation: Replace the hand-constructed pool plus regex scan with a test that instantiates the real gateway factory using stub adapters/spawnFn and asserts the served runtime set is exactly one primary operative pool plus classifier, with no mode/soul/model/effort-derived runtime ids or spawn partitions.

findings.title: HOT/BOOT documentation is internally ambiguous about model changes
findings.recommendation: Clarify path-specific behavior: either model/effort are universally HOT via slash-inject, or the orchestrator/soul path still respawns on tier-model change. As written, the docs say model is HOT and also cite model-change respawn, which weakens the promised honest HOT-settings gap.
